summary: School of landscape painting in early 16th-century Germany/Austria, named after the Danube River. Members include Albrecht Altdorfer and Wolf Huber. Altdorfer created one of the earliest pure landscapes in oil (Landscape with Footbridge).

works.name: General
works.description: Early 16th-century school of landscape painting named after the Danube River. Key members: Albrecht Altdorfer, Wolf Huber. Altdorfer's Landscape with Footbridge is one of the earliest pure landscapes in oil.

examples: Altdorfer was part of a school of landscape painting named for one of these geographic features

clues.clue: Altdorfer painted one of the earliest pure landscapes in oil (Landscape with Footbridge)
examples: This other German artist painted what is considered one of the earliest pure landscapes on oil

comprehensive_summary: The Danube School was an early 16th-century school of landscape painting in Germany/Austria named after the Danube River. Its most notable member was Albrecht Altdorfer, who created Landscape with Footbridge, considered one of the first pure landscapes in Western oil painting. Wolf Huber was another member. The school parallels the American Hudson River School in being named after a river.

cards.front: Movement: early 16th-century German school of landscape painting named after a river
Movement: Albrecht Altdorfer was its leading member
